quote: In the midst of chaos, there is also opportunity. — Sun Tzu
Waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. Be one. — Marcus Aurelius
He who conquers himself is the mightiest warrior. — Confucius
The unexamined life is not worth living. — Socrates
Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity. — Seneca
Be kind, for everyone you meet is fighting a harder battle. — Plato
Courage, above all things, is the first quality of a warrior. — Carl von Clausewitz
Man suffers more in imagination than in reality. — Seneca
Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom. — Aristotle
Do not pray for an easy life, pray for the strength to endure a difficult one. — Bruce Lee
To know your Enemy, you must become your Enemy. — Sun Tzu
The best revenge is not to be like your enemy. — Marcus Aurelius
Wealth consists not in having great possessions, but in having few wants. — Epictetus
It is not death that a man should fear, but he should fear never beginning to live. — Marcus Aurelius
Appear weak when you are strong, and strong when you are weak. — Sun Tzu
Fortune favors the bold. — Latin Proverb
He who opens a school door, closes a prison. — Victor Hugo
We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit. — Aristotle
Even death is not to be feared by one who has lived wisely. — Buddha
Do what you can, with what you have, where you are. — Theodore Roosevelt
Opportunities multiply as they are seized. — Sun Tzu
The brave man is not he who does not feel afraid, but he who conquers that fear. — Nelson Mandela
A room without books is like a body without a soul. — Cicero
Throw me to the wolves and I will return leading the pack. — Seneca
Strategy without tactics is the slowest route to victory. Tactics without strategy is the noise before defeat. — Sun Tzu
The man who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones. — Confucius
Live as if you were to die tomorrow. Learn as if you were to live forever. — Mahatma Gandhi
First say to yourself what you would be; and then do what you have to do. — Epictetus
Victory is reserved for those who are willing to pay its price. — Sun Tzu
The soul becomes dyed with the color of its thoughts. — Marcus Aurelius
Knowledge without justice ought to be called cunning rather than wisdom. — Plato
What we achieve inwardly will change outer reality. — Plutarch
No man has a good enough memory to be a successful liar. — Abraham Lincoln
Poverty is the parent of revolution and crime. — Aristotle
Cowards die many times before their deaths; the valiant never taste of death but once. — Julius Caesar
The more you sweat in peace, the less you bleed in war. — Norman Schwarzkopf
We are more often frightened than hurt; and we suffer more in imagination than in reality. — Seneca
The greatest victory is to conquer yourself. — Aristotle
Wars begin in the minds of men. — UNESCO preamble
If you are far from the enemy, make him believe you are near. — Sun Tzu
He who has a why to live can bear almost any how. — Friedrich Nietzsche
Discipline is the soul of an army. — George Washington
Happiness depends upon ourselves. — Aristotle
Better to fight for something than live for nothing. — George S. Patton
An ounce of practice is worth more than tons of preaching. — Mahatma Gandhi
The way is not in the sky. The way is in the heart. — Buddha
History is written by the victors. — Winston Churchill
The supreme art of war is to subdue the enemy without fighting. — Sun Tzu
Liberty means responsibility. That is why most men dread it. — George Bernard Shaw
A man who dares to waste one hour of time has not discovered the value of life. — Charles Darwin
Do not go where the path may lead, go instead where there is no path and leave a trail. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Live life as though nobody is watching, and express yourself as though everyone is listening. — Nelson Mandela
Power tends to corrupt, and absolute power corrupts absolutely. — Lord Acton
He who is not courageous enough to take risks will accomplish nothing in life. — Muhammad Ali
Glory is fleeting, but obscurity is forever. — Napoleon Bonaparte
Be as you wish to seem. — Socrates
No man is free who is not a master of himself. — Epictetus
Do not dwell in the past, do not dream of the future, concentrate the mind on the present moment. — Buddha
There is nothing impossible to him who will try. — Alexander the Great
Perseverance, secret of all triumphs. — Victor Hugo
Freedom is nothing else but a chance to be better. — Albert Camus
We must all suffer one of two things: the pain of discipline or the pain of regret. — Jim Rohn
Bravery is the solution to regret. — Robin Sharma
The future depends on what you do today. — Mahatma Gandhi
Fall seven times and stand up eight. — Japanese Proverb
The wise man does at once what the fool does finally. — Niccolò Machiavelli
It always seems impossible until it’s done. — Nelson Mandela
Silence is a true friend who never betrays. — Confucius
Our life is what our thoughts make it. — Marcus Aurelius
Do not wait to strike till the iron is hot, but make it hot by striking. — William Butler Yeats
Action is the foundational key to all success. — Pablo Picasso
You must be the change you wish to see in the world. — Mahatma Gandhi
A hero is no braver than an ordinary man, but he is brave five minutes longer. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
The harder the conflict, the greater the triumph. — George Washington
Do not go gentle into that good night. — Dylan Thomas
It is not length of life, but depth of life. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
We are masters of the unsaid words, but slaves of those we let slip out. — Winston Churchill
Victory belongs to the most persevering. — Napoleon Bonaparte
A leader is one who knows the way, goes the way, and shows the way. — John C. Maxwell
Do not let what you cannot do interfere with what you can do. — John Wooden
Experience is the teacher of all things. — Julius Caesar
An army of sheep led by a lion is better than an army of lions led by a sheep. — Alexander the Great
Strength does not come from physical capacity. It comes from an indomitable will. — Mahatma Gandhi
Character is destiny. — Heraclitus
A warrior is worthless unless he rises above others and stands strong in the midst of a storm. — Yamamoto Tsunetomo
There is nothing more powerful than an idea whose time has come. — Victor Hugo
To improve is to change; to be perfect is to change often. — Winston Churchill
Energy and persistence conquer all things. — Benjamin Franklin
Success is how high you bounce when you hit bottom. — George S. Patton
What we think, we become. — Buddha
